element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns paddle control and basic tile positioning in well
Core objective taught immediately; simple horizontal/vertical/diagonal line formation
5-tile paddle capacity creates strategic decisions about tile placement order
Bonus point system rewards pattern optimization and cascading matches
Falling tiles create new matches; bonus points for cascades formed by placement

element_introduction_order.element: Wave selection branching (choice of next wave)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player selects from 3 difficulty options; later waves grant bonus points but more drops
Tiles falling off conveyor or paddle overflow penalize player; drop meter limits failures

element_introduction_order.element: Well capacity constraint (5 columns × 5 rows = 25 tiles max)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Spatial resource management; game ends if well fills completely

element_introduction_order.element: Tile flip mechanic (up joystick to flip top paddle tile backward)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Advanced positioning technique for tile sequencing

element_introduction_order.element: Varied wave objectives (Klax count, point targets, survival limits)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Each wave specifies unique goal; forces adaptive strategy

element_introduction_order.element: End-of-wave bonus scoring (tiles remaining on conveyor/paddle, empty well spaces)

typical_relief_triggers: Successfully forming a Klax causes tiles to disappear
Cascade chains trigger multiple automatic clears
Wave completion and drop meter reset
Completing end-of-wave bonus (empty spaces in well)
typical_tension_triggers: Well filling up toward capacity limit
Conveyor belt advancing without catching tiles
Wave objectives approaching deadline or requiring high point thresholds
Tiles advancing down conveyor faster than player can react
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating intensity across 100-wave structure; player chooses difficulty escalation every 5 waves; tile speed and objective complexity increase through later waves; cascading matches provide explosive relief punctuation

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory waves teach basic catching and Klax formation with achievable objectives; drop meter replenishes after every 5 waves allowing learning window
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Wave objectives intensify; player must choose between safe early waves (fewer bonus points) or challenging later waves (higher risk/reward); cascade mechanics and well management become critical
difficulty_curve.late_game: Later waves (75-100) likely feature high tile velocity, strict point/Klax requirements, and resource constraint pressure; player exhausts available drops or completes all 100 waves
notable_spikes: Every 5th wave transition (player must choose next difficulty level)
Mid-game wave selection pushing toward harder branches
Late-game waves with survival or high point requirements

notable_level_patterns.description: Game structure groups waves into 20 sets of 5 waves. After completing each 5-wave set, drop meter resets and player chooses among 3 difficulty options for next cluster. Creates rhythmic difficulty negotiation.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Waves 1-5 (introductory cluster), Wave 5 choice point, Waves 6-10 (second cluster)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each wave specifies unique goal type: fixed number of Klaxs to form, point score threshold, or tile survival count. Forces strategic adaptation rather than repetitive execution.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Wave objectives vary across all 100 waves

notable_level_patterns.description: Clearing tiles causes gravity-based chain reactions. Single strategic placement can trigger multiple simultaneous Klax formations, rewarding foresight and spatial visualization.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any wave; cascades become more profitable in later waves
